베드로후서 2:1.content: But false prophets also arose among the people, as false teachers will also be among you, who will secretly bring in destructive heresies, denying even the Master who bought them, bringing on themselves swift destruction.

베드로후서 2:2.content: Many will follow their immoral ways, and as a result, the way of the truth will be maligned.

베드로후서 2:3.content: In covetousness they will exploit you with deceptive words: whose sentence now from of old doesn’t linger, and their destruction will not slumber.

베드로후서 2:4.content: For if God didn’t spare angels when they sinned, but cast them down to Tartarus, and committed them to pits of darkness to be reserved for judgment;

베드로후서 2:5.content: and didn’t spare the ancient world, but preserved Noah with seven others, a preacher of righteousness, when he brought a flood on the world of the ungodly;

베드로후서 2:6.content: and turning the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah into ashes, condemned them to destruction, having made them an example to those who would live in an ungodly way;

베드로후서 2:7.content: and delivered righteous Lot, who was very distressed by the lustful life of the wicked

베드로후서 2:8.content: (for that righteous man dwelling among them was tormented in his righteous soul from day to day with seeing and hearing lawless deeds):

베드로후서 2:9.content: the Lord knows how to deliver the godly out of temptation and to keep the unrighteous under punishment for the day of judgment,

베드로후서 2:10.content: but chiefly those who walk after the flesh in the lust of defilement and despise authority. Daring, self-willed, they are not afraid to speak evil of dignitaries;

베드로후서 2:11.content: whereas angels, though greater in might and power, don’t bring a railing judgment against them before the Lord.

베드로후서 2:12.content: But these, as unreasoning creatures, born natural animals to be taken and destroyed, speaking evil in matters about which they are ignorant, will in their destroying surely be destroyed,

베드로후서 2:13.content: receiving the wages of unrighteousness; people who count it pleasure to revel in the daytime, spots and defects, reveling in their deceit while they feast with you;

베드로후서 2:14.content: having eyes full of adultery, and who can’t cease from sin; enticing unsettled souls; having a heart trained in greed; children of cursing;

베드로후서 2:15.content: forsaking the right way, they went astray, having followed the way of Balaam the son of Beor, who loved the wages of wrongdoing;

베드로후서 2:16.content: but he was rebuked for his own disobedience. A mute donkey spoke with a man’s voice and stopped the madness of the prophet.

베드로후서 2:17.content: These are wells without water, clouds driven by a storm; for whom the blackness of darkness has been reserved forever.

베드로후서 2:18.content: For, uttering great swelling words of emptiness, they entice in the lusts of the flesh, by licentiousness, those who are indeed escaping from those who live in error;

베드로후서 2:19.content: promising them liberty, while they themselves are bondservants of corruption; for a man is brought into bondage by whoever overcomes him.

베드로후서 2:20.content: For if, after they have escaped the defilement of the world through the knowledge of the Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, they are again entangled in it and overcome, the last state has become worse for them than the first.

베드로후서 2:21.content: For it would be better for them not to have known the way of righteousness, than after knowing it, to turn back from the holy commandment delivered to them.

베드로후서 2:22.content: But it has happened to them according to the true proverb, “The dog turns to his own vomit again,” and “the sow that has washed to wallowing in the mire.”
